---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[

Simulating the bargaining model of war

]]

<br>
<br>
<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for class
1. Print bargaining cards in Google drive
2. Open Wednesday notes for tracking results
3. Are you giving extra credit to the winner each game?
  + YES, but only 1 max per person per day

<br>

As you come in make sure you get cards from me.

Everyone will need paper for tracking their strength and something to write with.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/06_1-WW1.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

This week we start to grapple with a HUGE question in IR: Why do wars happen?

<br>
It's actually a tougher question to answer than you might think.

Given that they are hugely costly in terms of blood and treasure, why would any country actively decide to use war to get what it wants?

#### Take World War 1
+ Lasts four years and involves some 30 countries
+ Nearly 10 million soldiers killed,
+ 21 million wounded,
+ Costs hundreds of billions of dollars,
+ Leads to the collapse of four empires: the Russian Empire in 1917, the German and the Austro-Hungarian in 1918, and the Ottoman in 1922.
+ The Bolsheviks took power in Russia in 1917, and fascists triumphed in Italy in 1922.
+ Encourages or allows the flu epidemic that killed 25 million around the world
+ and on, and on.


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/06_1-WW1-2.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

And then 20 years later we decided to do it all again!

WW2 kills more people, costs more money and destroys more of the global economy.

Not to mention inspires the invention of nukes...

<br>

It is the incredible death and destruction of WW1 and WW2 that leads to the birth of international relations as a subfield of political science.

The idea is to understand these horrible events so we can stop them.

And "understanding" means developing models!


---

background-image: url('libs/Images/06_1-kennedy-khruschev2.png')
background-size: 84%
background-position: bottom

.textred[.size60[.center[**The Bargaining Model of War**]]]

???

This week we will explore what has come to be called "the bargaining model of war."

James Fearon in 1995 published an article titled, "Rationalist Explanations for War."

In it he offers a really interesting model of war as a bargaining process.

Two sides with opposing goals each trying to get what they want without having to pay too high a cost to get it.

<br>

My plan for today is that each of you experience the dynamics of the model before we analyze it on Wednesday.


---

class: middle, slideorange

.size45[.center[**Simulating the Bargaining Model of War**]]

.size40[
+ Two Players

+ Five Rounds

+ "Battle Strength" = sum of numbered cards

+ "Resources" are measured in pennies

+ Objective: End the game with the most pennies
]

???

* Because of covid everyone has their own cards and we won't be trading pennies *

* Please track your "pennies" on a piece of paper. *

* Everyone starts with 10 pennies *

<br>

Ok, how do we play?

It's pretty simple...


---

```{r, fig.retina=3, fig.align='center', fig.asp=0.618, out.width='95%', fig.width=6}
d <- tibble(
    x = 1:10,
    y = 1:10
)

d |> ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y)) +
         geom_blank() +
         coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,10), ylim = c(0,10)) +
         theme_void() +
         scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         scale_y_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         annotate("text", x = 1, y = 9.5, label = "The Penny War", size=5) +
         annotate("text", x = 5, y = 9.5, label = "Ante up:\nBoth pay -1 and\ndraw 3 cards", size=4.5)
```

???

1. Ante up.
- This represents the cost of being a sovereign, functioning state.

Both players ante up 1 "penny" into the pot, and draw four cards from their deck.

**covid:** So, on your piece of paper subtract one from your total at the start of the game.


---

```{r, fig.retina=3, fig.align='center', fig.asp=0.618, out.width='95%', fig.width=6}
d |> ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y)) +
         geom_blank() +
         coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,10), ylim = c(0,10)) +
         theme_void() +
         scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         scale_y_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         annotate("text", x = 1, y = 9.5, label = "The Penny War", size=5) +
         annotate("text", x = 5, y = 9.5, label = "Ante up:\nBoth pay -1 and\ndraw 3 cards", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 6, label = "P1's Choice:", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 6, label = "Give up\n(P2 gets +2)", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 7.5, y = 6, label = "Declare war", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 3.5, y = 8, yend = 6.5, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))  +
         annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 6.5, y = 8, yend = 6.5, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))
```

???

2. P1 Choice: Give up or fight!

- If P1 concedes, P2 gets the pot and the round ends

- If P1 demands the pot (war), P2 gets a choice...

### Make sense?



---

```{r, fig.retina=3, fig.align='center', fig.asp=0.618, out.width='95%', fig.width=6}
d |> ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y)) +
         geom_blank() +
         coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,10), ylim = c(0,10)) +
         theme_void() +
         scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         scale_y_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         annotate("text", x = 1, y = 9.5, label = "The Penny War", size=5) +
         annotate("text", x = 5, y = 9.5, label = "Ante up:\nBoth pay -1 and\ndraw 3 cards", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 6, label = "P1's Choice:", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 6, label = "Give up\n(P2 gets +2)", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 7.5, y = 6, label = "Declare war", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 3.5, y = 8, yend = 6.5, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))  +
         annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 6.5, y = 8, yend = 6.5, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in"))) +
         annotate("text", x = 4, y = 3, label = "P2's Choice:", color = "green4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 6, y = 3, label = "Give up\n(P1 gets +2)", color = "green4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 9, y = 3, label = "Declare war", color = "green4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("segment", x = 7.5, xend = 6.5, y = 5.5, yend = 3.75, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))  +
         annotate("segment", x = 7.5, xend = 8.5, y = 5.5, yend = 3.75, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))
```

???

3. Choice shifts to P2: Give up or fight!

- If P2 concedes, P1 gets the pot and the round ends

- If P2 resists, you go to war!

### Make sense?



---

```{r, fig.retina=3, fig.align='center', fig.asp=0.618, out.width='95%', fig.width=6}
d |> ggplot(aes(x = x, y = y)) +
         geom_blank() +
         coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,10), ylim = c(0,10)) +
         theme_void() +
         scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         scale_y_continuous(breaks = 0:10) +
         annotate("text", x = 1, y = 9.5, label = "The Penny War", size=5) +
         annotate("text", x = 5, y = 9.5, label = "Ante up:\nBoth pay -1 and\ndraw 3 cards", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 6, label = "P1's Choice:", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 2.5, y = 6, label = "Give up\n(P2 gets +2)", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 7.5, y = 6, label = "Declare war", color = "blue4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 3.5, y = 8, yend = 6.5, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))  +
         annotate("segment", x = 5, xend = 6.5, y = 8, yend = 6.5, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in"))) +
         annotate("text", x = 4, y = 3, label = "P2's Choice:", color = "green4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 6, y = 3, label = "Give up\n(P1 gets +2)", color = "green4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("text", x = 9, y = 3, label = "Declare war", color = "green4", size=4.5) +
         annotate("segment", x = 7.5, xend = 6.5, y = 5.5, yend = 3.75, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in")))  +
         annotate("segment", x = 7.5, xend = 8.5, y = 5.5, yend = 3.75, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.1, "in"))) +
         annotate("text", x = 5, y = 1, label = "War: Both pay costs of war (-1) and strongest hand wins (+2)", size = 5.25, color = "red1")
```

???

4. War

Both players declare war

Cost of war: One penny is lost in the fighting.

**covid:** So, on your piece of paper subtract one from your total.

You have to pay for the tanks and losses to your economy!

Show your cards, stronger side wins

### Make sense?

<br>

Each round you'll alternate being P1 and we'll play five rounds.

### Any question on how the game works?

<br>

Let's see it in action once.

I need two volunteers.

Both stand up and shuffle your cards.

Ok, both of you ante up one penny so you are down to 9 total.

Now draw three cards.

*You pick P1*

Ok, P1 what do you want to do?

*Push them to war so we can see it all the way through*

*Play it out*

<br>

### Everybody clear on how to play the game?




---

class: slideorange, middle

.size45[.center[**Simulating the Bargaining Model of War**]]

.size40[
1. Find an opponent

2. Make sure you have 
	+ 10 pennies (or paper/pen), and 
	+ 10 numbered cards

3. Complete five rounds
	+ Who won overall?
	+ How many wars did you fight?
]

???

Alternate who is P1 each round and keep score.

*COVID* To maintain social distancing, please also use the hallway.

<br>

Ok, play the game.

*Back to our seats!*

Each pair tell us your final score and how many wars you fought.

*YOU RECORD ON NOTES, not the board yet: sum of all wars in class that round

<br>

Ok, let's change it up this time.

Everybody is back to ten pennies.



---

class: slideorange, middle

.size45[.center[**Simulating the Bargaining Model of War**]

<br>

**Version 2**

+ Find a new opponent, and

+ Keep **ONE** card face up each round.
]

???

Change partners and get ready to play again.

THIS TIME, Keep ONE card face up each round.

### Everybody clear on the rule change?

Start of game: Pay the ante, draw your cards and reveal one of them to your opponent.

Go!

*Back to our seats!*

Each pair tell us your final score and how many wars you fought.

*YOU RECORD ON NOTES, not the board yet: sum of all wars in class that round 




---

class: slideorange, middle

.size45[.center[**Simulating the Bargaining Model of War**]

<br>

**Version 3**

+ Find a new opponent, and

+ Keep **TWO** cards face up each round.
]

???

Do it again BUT this time you have to keep TWO of your cards face up the whole time.

### Everybody clear on the rule change?

Go!

* Back to our seats! *

Each pair tell us your final score and how many wars you fought.

* YOU RECORD ON NOTES, not the board yet: sum of all wars in class that round 




---

class: slideorange, middle

.size45[.center[**Simulating the Bargaining Model of War**]

<br>

**Version 4**

+ Find a new opponent, and

+ Keep **ALL** cards face up each round.
]

???

Last time, BUT now you have to keep ALL of your cards face up the whole time.

### Everybody clear on the rule change?

Go!

* Back to our seats! *

Each pair tell us your final score and how many wars you fought.

* YOU RECORD ON NOTES, not the board yet: sum of all wars in class that round 

<br>

** ON BOARD: Total wars per round **




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/06_1-kennedy-khruschev2.png')
background-size: 84%
background-position: bottom

.textred[.size60[.center[**The Bargaining Model of War**]]]

???

Alright, let's talk about what just happened.

### What do we learn from this simulation?


### Talk to me about how you played.
#### - Why did you play the way you did?

### Anybody try bluffing? Why or why not?

### Did anybody go to war with a weak hand? Why?

### In what ways is this a useful model of war?

### In what ways do you think it is a problematic model of war?
